title: Onboard a new developer
problem: A new developer is joining the team. Create a user account for them, give them sudo access, and set up SSH key authentication so they can log in without a password.

setup.description: Generate a throwaway SSH key pair to simulate the developer sending you their public key.
setup.note: Works on any Linux machine or WSL. No VPS needed.

hints: On Ubuntu/Debian use adduser — it is interactive and friendlier than useradd. On CentOS/RHEL use useradd -m -s /bin/bash
sudo group is for Ubuntu/Debian. On CentOS/RHEL the group is called wheel — usermod -aG wheel developer
SSH key auth means putting the public key in /home/developer/.ssh/authorized_keys — permissions on that folder are strict and will silently fail if wrong
solution:
1. Create user:
   Ubuntu/Debian:  sudo adduser developer
   CentOS/RHEL:    sudo useradd -m -s /bin/bash developer
   # adduser is interactive and sets up the home directory automatically. useradd needs -m to create it.

2. Add to sudo group:
   Ubuntu/Debian:  sudo usermod -aG sudo developer
   CentOS/RHEL:    sudo usermod -aG wheel developer
   # The sudo group name differs by distro — sudo on Debian/Ubuntu, wheel on RHEL/CentOS.

3. Create SSH directory:
   sudo mkdir -p /home/developer/.ssh
   # SSH requires this directory to exist. The -p flag prevents an error if it already exists.

4. Copy public key:
   sudo cp ~/.ssh/dev_key.pub /home/developer/.ssh/authorized_keys
   # Puts the developer's public key where SSH looks for it — the file must be named authorized_keys.

5. Fix permissions:
   sudo chmod 700 /home/developer/.ssh
   sudo chmod 600 /home/developer/.ssh/authorized_keys
   # SSH silently refuses keys if permissions are too open — 700 for the dir, 600 for the key file.

6. Fix ownership:
   sudo chown -R developer:developer /home/developer/.ssh
   # The developer must own their own .ssh directory, not root — otherwise SSH ignores the key.

7. Verify login:
   su - developer
   # Switch to the new account to confirm everything is working before handing over credentials.
